summary: a3s-code fetches and loads a native wheel from a publisher-mismatched GitHub org on import
details: On `import a3s_code`, `_bootstrap.ensure_native_loaded()` downloads a wheel from `https://github.com/A3S-Lab/Code/releases/download/v<version>/...whl`, extracts an embedded `_native.*` compiled extension into `~/.cache/a3s-code/<version>/`, and loads it as `a3s_code._native` via `ExtensionFileLoader`, executing arbitrary native code in the installer's process. The declared publisher across PKG-INFO, README, and pyproject.toml is `github.com/AI45Lab/Code`, a different organization from the `A3S-Lab/Code` org hardcoded in `_bootstrap.py`. The sha256 manifest used for integrity verification is fetched from the same `A3S-Lab/Code` path, so verification is self-attesting against whatever that org serves. The visual similarity between `AI45Lab` and `A3S-Lab`, together with a cover story about PyPI storage limits, conceals the redirection from casual review. Any environment that imports `a3s_code` at version 5.2.8 loads a native extension supplied by an organization other than the one the package documentation identifies as the publisher.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
